Cats vs. Dogs

发表于2022-07-23|更新于2022-07-24|AI
|阅读量:

使用CNN Keras建立模型进行图像识别¶

In [ ]:
#数据：https://www.kaggle.com/c/dogs-vs-cats/data
In [ ]:
import tensorflow as tf
In [ ]:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
# 背景设置为黑色
plt.style.use('dark_background')
import cv2
In [ ]:
from keras.models import Sequential

from keras.layers import Convolution2D
from keras.layers import MaxPooling2D

from keras.layers import Flatten
from keras.layers import Dense
In [ ]:
plt.figure(figsize = (12,6))
for i in range(1, 16, 1):
    img = cv2.imread('G:/dogs-vs-cats/train/dog/dog.'+str(i)+'.jpg')
    img = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB ) 
    plt.subplot(4,4,i)
    plt.imshow(img)
plt.show()
In [ ]:
plt.figure(figsize = (12,6))
for i in range(1, 16, 1):
    img = cv2.imread('G:/dogs-vs-cats/train/cat/cat.'+str(i)+'.jpg')
    img = cv2.cvtColor(img, cv2.COLOR_BGR2RGB ) 
    plt.subplot(4,4,i)
    plt.imshow(img)
plt.show()
In [ ]:
#build
model = Sequential()
model.add(Convolution2D(32 , 3, 3,  input_shape = (64, 64, 3), activation = 'relu'))
model.add(MaxPooling2D(pool_size  = (2,2)))
model.add(Flatten())
model.add(Dense(128, activation = 'relu'))
model.add(Dense(1, activation = 'sigmoid'))
In [ ]:
#compile
model.compile(optimizer = 'adam', loss = 'binary_crossentropy', metrics = ['accuracy'])
In [ ]:
# ImageDataGenerator 函数通过迭代图像来增强图像，因为 CNN 正准备处理该图像
from keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator

train_datagen = ImageDataGenerator(rescale = 1./255,
                                   shear_range = 0.2,
                                   zoom_range = 0.2,
                                   horizontal_flip = True)

test_datagen = ImageDataGenerator(rescale = 1./255)  #Image normalization.

train_set = train_datagen.flow_from_directory('G:/dogs-vs-cats/train/',
                                                 target_size = (64, 64),
                                                 batch_size = 32,
                                                 class_mode = 'binary')



test_set = test_datagen.flow_from_directory('G:/dogs-vs-cats/test1/',
                                            target_size = (64, 64),
                                            batch_size = 32,
                                            class_mode = 'binary')

Found 25000 images belonging to 2 classes.
Found 12500 images belonging to 1 classes.
In [ ]:
train_set.class_indices
Out[ ]:
{'cat': 0, 'dog': 1}
In [ ]:
test_set.class_indices
Out[ ]:
{'test1': 0}

len(train_set)
Out[ ]:
782
In [ ]:
len(test_set)
Out[ ]:
391
In [ ]:
model.fit_generator(train_set,
                         steps_per_epoch = len(train_set),
                         epochs = 1,
                         validation_data = test_set,
                         validation_steps = len(test_set))

C:\Users\Administrator\AppData\Local\Temp\2\ipykernel_8792\3093498578.py:1: UserWarning: `Model.fit_generator` is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Please use `Model.fit`, which supports generators.
  model.fit_generator(train_set,

782/782 [==============================] - 253s 323ms/step - loss: 0.6212 - accuracy: 0.6540 - val_loss: 1.3794 - val_accuracy: 0.2592
Out[ ]:
<keras.callbacks.History at 0x21e0c8c0a90>
In [ ]:
from tensorflow.keras.models import load_model
#模型保存
model.save('cat_dog.h5')
In [ ]:
######
from tensorflow.keras.models import load_model
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing import image
from tensorflow.keras.applications.vgg16 import preprocess_input
import numpy as np
model = load_model( 'cat_dog.h5')
In [ ]:
img = image.load_img('G:/dogs-vs-cats/test1/test1/9.jpg',target_size=(64,64))
x = image.img_to_array(img)
x = np.expand_dims(x,axis=0)
img_data = preprocess_input(x)
classes = model.predict(img_data)

print(classes)

1/1 [==============================] - 0s 95ms/step
[[0.]]

model.predict(img_data)

1/1 [==============================] - 0s 18ms/step

x1 = image.img_to_array(img1)
x1 = np.expand_dims(x1,axis=0)
img_data1 = preprocess_input(x1)
classes = model.predict(img_data1)

print(classes)
